scope.summary: F-LOW AU3 verified already satisfied: RekorAnchorReceipt::verify_offline parses the supported entry schema and requires exact equality in the authoritative artifact-hash field. This close records verification, not new work.
out_of_scope: Any code change. The finding describes a substring match that no longer exists on the trust path; re-implementing it would have been duplicate work.
The checkpoint/SET signature verification, which is delegated to the independently trusted RekorCheckpointVerifier and is outside this finding.

entry_points.name: RekorAnchorReceipt::verify_offline
entry_points.trace: verify_entry_binds_manifest (rekor.rs:207-249) dispatches on `kind`, deserializes the rekord/hashedrekord or dsse schema with #[serde(deny_unknown_fields)], pins apiVersion 0.0.1, and admits only `artifact_hash.algorithm == "sha256" && artifact_hash.value == expected_manifest_sha256`; every other kind returns HeadNotBoundToEntry. The DSSE arm deliberately uses payloadHash rather than envelopeHash. 32 rekor tests pass, covering all four negative-acceptance cases the bead names: digest outside the authoritative field, a hash superstring, duplicate conflicting hash fields (MalformedProof), and non-JSON (MalformedProof), plus the positive exact-hashedrekord and exact-DSSE paths.

entry_points.name: grep for the substring path
entry_points.trace: The only remaining ASCII-substring helper, contains_ascii (rekor.rs:505), is #[cfg(test)] and is referenced solely by its own unit test at line 1201 — it cannot participate in trust evaluation.

limitations: Verification of pre-existing state. I read the cited code and ran the suite; I did not re-derive when or in which commit the substring match was replaced.
Duplicate JSON keys are rejected as MalformedProof by the existing test's expectation; I did not separately audit serde_json's duplicate-key behaviour beyond confirming that assertion passes.
Scoped verification: cargo test -p oraclemcp-audit --lib rekor. No workspace-wide run.
